markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Document product purpose, core workflows, startup/run commands, expected inputs/outputs, and navigation paths. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualPurposeAndWorkflowPages leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualPurposeAndWorkflowPages is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 fcf9bce3. Purpose/workflow corpus seeded as PostgreSQL authority rows (src/user_manual/seed.rs): handshake-product-purpose (product + authority model + unified-work-surface law 7.1.1.9), core-workflows (index / code-nav / rich-doc author / Notes / retrieval / manual operation - each step a real route), startup-and-run-commands (verified run command 'cargo run -p handshake_core --bin handshake_core --features app-runtime', bind 127.0.0.1:37501, / + /api mounts, /health probe, managed PG 5544 + Handshake_Artifacts/managed_pgdata, scoped test commands), backend-navigation-and-identity (x-hsk-* header contract incl. which groups REQUIRE headers vs anonymous bootstrap surfaces, route namespace map, machine-readable header JSON), permissions-and-safety, manual-toc (the navigation root), kernel-write-governance (kernel002 manual imported deterministically per UMMIG-002). Tests GREEN on real PG: user_manual_content_tests::mt196_kernel002_manual_topics_are_seeded_as_pages (every kernel002 topic + every instruction line verbatim in the seeded page), mt196_documented_startup_constants_match_product_source (documented 37501/5544/managed_pgdata/'/api' nest pinned against src/main.rs + src/managed_postgres.rs - changing the product forces the manual update), lib seed tests (toc_links_every_page_and_every_page_is_reachable: BFS proves every page reachable from manual-toc; no dangling page_link).

concerns_found_and_fixed: Startup-claim verifiability: a test cannot bind main's hardcoded production listener, so runtime introspection of 37501 is impossible from the suite -> consistency test pins the documented constants against the product SOURCE files instead and the declaration is recorded in the test doc-comment (honest scope: source-pinned, not socket-proven; the /health probe path is documented for live verification).

Identity-stance accuracy: documents API defaults absent actor-kind to least-privileged unauthenticated while code-nav defaults to system - the navigation page documents the contract per surface group instead of one false generalization.
residual_risks: Workflow prose could drift semantically even when routes still exist; mitigations: every named route is anchor-joined (freshness) + runtime-probed (mtdoc), and MT-204 stale_content verdicts fire on any seed change without resync.

adversarial_review_v2.summary: Follow-up adversarial review found no blocking findings after durable ModelManual-to-UserManual wording, USER_MANUAL_VERSION HBR-MAN-001 guidance, Loom route parity, full-row freshness, receipt atomicity, and fail-closed PostgreSQL test fixes.

hardening_closed.note: State recovered to READY_FOR_VALIDATION only. completed_by remains null; validator owns PASS/COMPLETED.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-196 requires a built-in UserManual product surface documenting product purpose, core workflows, startup/run commands, expected inputs/outputs, and backend navigation/identity as durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code seeds these as real PG-persisted UserManual pages (seed.rs) with constants pinned against live product source, a kernel002 verbatim import, and BFS TOC reachability, all proven by tests that connect to and round-trip through a real PostgreSQL cluster.
findings: seed.rs:195-198,260,316,404,483,223 seeds the exact required pages: handshake-product-purpose, core-workflows, startup-and-run-commands, backend-navigation-and-identity, plus manual-toc navigation root and kernel-write-governance/permissions pages — directly satisfying the MT scope (purpose/workflows/startup/navigation).

ensure_seeded (seed.rs:1809-1848) persists the corpus to real PostgreSQL via UserManualStore::upsert_page -> 'INSERT INTO user_manual_pages' (store.rs:350); migration 0310_user_manual.sql creates pages/sections/anchors/tools/features/versions/aliases with EventLedger FK receipt columns (KNOWLEDGE_USER_MANUAL_ENTRY_RECORDED). This is real Handshake-native durable authority, not SQLite/in-memory/markdown-vault.

sub_rule_2 (real resource touch) PASS: tests/user_manual_support.rs:96-118 builds AppState whose storage AND pool connect to the isolated real-PG schema (PostgresDatabase::connect + PgPoolOptions::connect); the NoopRecorder/NoopLlmClient are only for flight-recorder/LLM, NOT the durable manual store. skip_if_no_pg! (support.rs:132-146) PANICS when PG is unavailable — it does not silently skip, so the proof cannot pass without a live cluster.

mt196_kernel002_manual_topics_are_seeded_as_pages (content_tests.rs:54-81) re-queries get_page_by_slug from live PG and asserts every kernel002 legacy topic + every instruction line is present verbatim — a true durable round-trip, deterministic UMMIG-002 import (seed.rs:1294-1326).

mt196_documented_startup_constants_match_product_source (content_tests.rs:89-126) pins documented 127.0.0.1:37501 / 5544 / managed_pgdata / '/api' nest against real src/main.rs and src/managed_postgres.rs — changing the product forces a manual update. Honest scope (source-pinned, /health documented for live probe), not a fake/socket-faked claim.
sub_rule_1 (no deferred-live escape) PASS: grep of src/user_manual/** found zero todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable placeholders on any path.

sub_rule_3 PASS: lifecycle.completed_by is null; claimed_by/completed_by are not the same actor self-certifying — validator owns the COMPLETED transition. Adversarial self-review verdicts carry no authority here.

Spec anchors cited by the code (10.15.8 UserManual bridge, 2.3.13.11 UserManualRecord, 12.7 PRIM-UserManual) all resolve in current Master Spec v02.194 (spec-modules 02/10/12). minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
